title: Run K-fold cross-validated HB fits and finalize all folds in one call
summary: Combines `cv-fit` and `cv-finalize` into a single command: runs HB MCMC across all folds in a `choice-cv split` directory, then immediately finalizes each fold and writes the aggregate CV summary. Emits a single started / finished event pair.
description: Walks a `choice-cv split` directory and runs an HB fit on every fold via Metropolis-Hastings MCMC, then immediately finalizes each fold to produce per-fold exports. After all folds complete, writes `cv_summary.csv` and `cv_summary.json`. Equivalent to calling `cv-fit` then `cv-finalize` back-to-back, but in a single protocol invocation. The raw per-fold chains are preserved so you can still call `cv-finalize` or `cv-fit-extend` if needed.
usage:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::cv-fit-finalize"]]
folds_dir = "<cv-folds dir>"
run_dir = "<output dir>"
run_name = "hbmh_run"
# optional:
segments = 1
iterations = 10000
burnin = 0.2
threads = 4

arguments.name: folds_dir
arguments.description: Directory containing per-fold subdirectories. Each subdirectory must contain `train.csv` and `test.csv` files in coded long format.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory. Created if it does not exist. Each fold gets its own subdirectory, and `cv_summary.csv` plus `cv_summary.json` are written at the top level.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: export_dir
arguments.description: Where to write per-fold CSV exports. Defaults to `{run_dir}/{fold_id}/exports`.

arguments.name: covariate_input
arguments.description: Optional respondent-level covariate CSV applied uniformly to all folds.

arguments.name: segments
arguments.description: Number of mixture components. Default `2`. Applied to every fold.

arguments.name: iterations
arguments.description: Total MCMC iterations per fold. Default `10000`.

arguments.name: save_every
arguments.description: Save draws every N iterations. Default `10`.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of threads per fold. Default `4`.

arguments.name: random_seed
arguments.description: Random seed (`0` = non-deterministic). Each fold derives its own seed from the base seed.

arguments.name: chains
arguments.description: Number of independent MCMC chains per fold. Default `1`. Each chain uses a seed derived from `random_seed` and its chain index. Draws are pooled at finalize time within each fold.

arguments.name: report_thin
arguments.description: Convergence-report snapshot interval in saved draws per fold. Default `0` (same as `checkpoint_every`). Controls chart data-point density.

arguments.name: burnin
arguments.description: Burnin fraction discarded when computing per-fold summaries. Default `0.2`.

arguments.name: attribute_signs
arguments.description: Sign constraints applied uniformly to all folds: `{ name, rule }` where `rule` is `"non_neg"` or `"non_pos"`.

details: `cv-fit-finalize` runs the complete HB cross-validation pipeline in a single call. Each fold is sampled via MH MCMC and immediately finalized before moving to the next fold. The aggregate CV summary is written after all folds complete. The raw per-fold chains are persisted so you can still `cv-finalize` separately to change the burnin.
value:
On success, `run_dir` contains:

- One subdirectory per fold, each containing the chain state and finalized exports (`point_estimates.csv`).
- `cv_summary.csv` and `cv_summary.json` at the top level.

In protocol mode the plugin emits a single `event.run.started`, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`) carrying the aggregate summary.
notes: Equivalent to calling `cv-fit` then `cv-finalize` with the same `run_dir`.
Per-fold chains are persisted — you can still call `cv-finalize` to change burnin or `cv-fit-extend` to add more iterations.

see_also.description: Produces the per-fold directory layout this command consumes.

examples.title: Basic HB CV fit-finalize (1 segment)
examples.body:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::cv-fit-finalize"]]
folds_dir = "./folds"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-cv"
run_name = "hbmh_run1"
segments = 1
iterations = 10000
burnin = 0.2

fields.name: folds_dir
fields.description: Directory containing per-fold subdirectories (each with train.csv + test.csv).

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory. Per-fold subdirs and cv_summary.csv are written here.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: export_dir
fields.description: Where to write per-fold CSV exports. Defaults to {fold_dir}/exports.

fields.name: covariate_input
fields.description: Optional covariate CSV applied to all folds.

fields.name: iterations
fields.description: Total MCMC iterations per fold. Default: 10000.

fields.name: save_every
fields.description: Save draws every N iterations. Default: 10.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Number of threads per fold. Default: 4.

fields.name: chains
fields.description: Number of independent MCMC chains per fold. Default: 1.

fields.name: report_thin
fields.description: Snapshot every N saved draws per fold (0 = checkpoint_every). Default: 0.

fields.name: burnin
fields.description: Burnin fraction discarded when computing summaries. Default: 0.2.

fields.name: attribute_signs
fields.description: Sign constraints: [{name, rule}] where rule is non_neg or non_pos.

artifacts.title: Cross-validation summary
artifacts.description: Per-fold and aggregate holdout prediction accuracy. The headline metric for comparing HB against latent class and aggregate logit models.

fields.name: train_fit
fields.description: In-sample fit statistic for the fold.

fields.name: holdout_fit
fields.description: Out-of-sample prediction accuracy for the fold.

artifacts.title: Per-fold respondent partworths
artifacts.description: Individual-level preference scores estimated from each fold's training sample. One file per fold.

fields.name: <attribute_level_columns>
fields.description: Estimated partworth for each attribute level.
